text: My best friend is a person who always listens to me.
Today you do not just answer questions. You have a real conversation.
Conversation
Respond
React
Clarify
Express
Naturally
Comfortable

In my opinion, mornings are the best time to study.
Tea is healthier than coffee, but coffee is the best in the morning.
You should rest, and you must not work so late.
She is the friend who lives in the house that has a beautiful view.
Sandra, you seem so much more comfortable in English now. What changed?
Thank you! In my opinion, the secret is practice. Now I respond naturally, instead of only saying yes or no. Do you agree?
Totally. Speaking is harder than reading, but it is also more rewarding. What do you find the most difficult?
Sometimes I do not understand a fast speaker, so I ask them to clarify. Do you think that is a good idea?
Definitely. You should always ask, and you must not pretend to understand. That is exactly what good speakers do.
That is a relief to hear. My favorite person to talk to is my granddaughter, who is patient and always follows up. She is the best teacher I have.
In my opinion, the best part of my week is
I think this is better than that, because
My favorite person to talk to is someone who
In my opinion, what do you think?
Could you clarify what you mean?
That is interesting. Tell me more about it.
I feel comfortable speaking naturally now.
Let me follow up: how did it go in the end?

Let me tell you what I think makes a good conversation. For me, the most important thing is to listen well. When the other person is speaking, I try not to think about my answer. I just listen. Then I respond naturally, with a real reaction. If I do not understand something, I ask the person to clarify. I never pretend. In my opinion, a good listener is more interesting than a great talker. I also like to follow up. If a friend tells me about a problem on Monday, I ask about it on Friday. That small question shows I care, and it makes the other person feel comfortable. A conversation is not a test. It is two people building something together, one honest answer at a time.

I want to tell you how my English has changed this year. At the start, I could only give answers of one word. Someone asked a question, and I said yes or no. I was not comfortable. But step by step, things got better. I learned to give my opinion and to say why. I learned to compare two things and choose the better one. I learned to give advice with should and must, and to describe the people and places I love. Now, when I have a real conversation, I do not freeze. I respond, I react, and I follow up with my own questions. For me, that is the biggest achievement: I do not just answer anymore. I talk, naturally, like myself.

First, listen carefully to what the other person says. Then respond naturally and show a real reaction. Next, if something is not clear, ask the person to clarify. After that, express your own opinion and say why. Finally, follow up with a question to keep the conversation going.

If I do not understand, I ask the person to clarify.
A few days later, I like to follow up with a question.
I feel comfortable talking in English now.
He asked a question, so I responded with a full sentence.
It is good to express your opinion clearly.

First, listen carefully to what the other person says. Then respond naturally and show a real reaction. Next, if something is not clear, ask the person to clarify. After that, express your own opinion and say why. Finally, follow up with a question to keep the conversation going.
In my opinion, this is the best choice. What do you think?
Could you clarify what you mean?
I feel comfortable speaking naturally now.

In my opinion, what do you think?
That is interesting. Tell me more about it.
